questions.question: What is the primary function of a node in a plant?

options.B: To produce new leaves and stems
options.C: To support the weight of the plant

questions.explanation: A node is a point on a stem where a leaf or branch originates. It is responsible for producing new leaves and stems.

questions.question: What type of roots are adapted to grow in water?

options.C: Pneumatophores

questions.explanation: Pneumatophores are specialized roots that grow in water to allow for gas exchange.

questions.question: What is the main characteristic of a simple leaf?

options.A: It has a long petiole and a flat blade
options.B: It has a short petiole and a narrow blade
options.C: It has no petiole and a wide blade
options.D: It is compound and has many leaflets

questions.explanation: A simple leaf has a single blade with a distinct petiole.

questions.question: What is the function of an internode in a plant?

options.B: To support the weight of the plant
options.C: To provide space for growth between nodes

questions.explanation: An internode is the section of stem between two nodes.

questions.question: What type of plant structure allows for gas exchange?

options.C: Lenticels

questions.explanation: Pneumatophores are specialized roots that allow for gas exchange in water.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a petiole?

options.B: To support the weight of the leaf
options.C: To attach the leaf to the stem

questions.explanation: A petiole is a stalk that attaches a leaf to the stem.

questions.question: What type of roots are adapted to grow in dry soil?

options.C: Pneumatophores

questions.explanation: Fibrous roots are adapted to grow in dry soil by spreading out widely.

questions.question: What is the main characteristic of a compound leaf?

options.A: It has many leaflets attached to a central stem
options.B: It has a single blade with a distinct petiole
options.C: It has no petiole and a wide blade
options.D: It is simple and has one leaflet

questions.explanation: A compound leaf has multiple leaflets attached to a central stem.

questions.question: What is the function of an apical bud?

options.B: To support the weight of the plant
options.C: To produce new leaves and stems

questions.explanation: An apical bud is responsible for producing new leaves and stems.

questions.question: What type of plant structure allows for water transport?

options.C: Stomata

questions.explanation: Xylem is responsible for transporting water and minerals throughout the plant.

questions.question: What is the main characteristic of a stolon?

options.A: It produces new plants at its tips
options.C: It has no petiole and a wide blade
options.D: It is simple and has one leaflet

questions.explanation: A stolon produces new plants at its tips.

questions.question: What type of roots are adapted to grow in water?

options.C: Pneumatophores

questions.explanation: Pneumatophores are specialized roots that grow in water to allow for gas exchange.
